text: First, 10 milliliters of distilled water is drawn into a syringe.
It is then instilled into the ICG vial, which is a powder, and then mixed.
Draw up 0.5 mL of prepared ICG into a syringe, preferably a 1 mL tuberculin syringe.
Instill 0.25 mL of ICG onto the coil while it is still in the original packaged sheath.
After 10 minutes, instill an additional 0.25 mL of ICG onto the coil prior to loading the coil into the deployment catheter.
The coil is then loaded into the superdimension catheter, pushing the coil from the cartridge sheath into the SuperD catheter.
The ICG dye-soaked coil is then deployed under fluoroscopic guidance at or near the lesion or nodule.
Here, the right upper lobe nodule is clearly marked by the small focal area of ICG.
